title: pithecellobium albicans
intro: Pithecellobium albicans, also known as the albida or tamarugo tree, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The extracts of the plant have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as possessing antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
